sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Vicor Corp CEO Vinciarelli sells $2.1m in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Vicor Corp CEO Patrizio Vinciarelli sold $2.1 million worth of company stock on December 22, 2025, involving 19,000 shares at prices ranging from $104.9823 to $106.76. Despite this sale, Vicor maintains strong financials and analysts anticipate revenue and net income growth. The stock, which has returned 125% over the past year, is currently deemed overvalued by InvestingPro.

feed.title: Focus Universal receives Nasdaq conditional compliance for continued listing
feed.summary: Focus Universal Inc. (NASDAQ:FCUV) has announced that it received a conditional compliance letter from Nasdaq for continued listing, having rectified its stockholders' equity deficiency. The company previously fell below Nasdaq's requirements but demonstrated compliance based on its December 17, 2025, Form 8-K filing. This conditional compliance allows FCUV to remain listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market, contingent on ongoing adherence to all other listing requirements.

feed.title: Sanofi pushes ahead on adult vaccines with $2.2 billion Dynavax deal
feed.summary: Sanofi announced its agreement to acquire U.S. biotech Dynavax Technologies for approximately $2.2 billion, adding an adult hepatitis B vaccine and a promising experimental shingles shot to its portfolio. This acquisition aims to diversify Sanofi's vaccine business, especially given new U.S. policy changes affecting childhood immunizations. The deal, expected to close in Q1 2026, will also bring Dynavax's Heplisav-B and an experimental shingles vaccine, Z-1018, into Sanofi's product pipeline to drive future revenue growth.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Stock After the Bell on Dec. 24, 2025: Shares Close at $910.78 — What to Know Before Markets Reopen
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs (GS) stock closed higher at $910.78 on a shortened Christmas Eve session, with shares largely unchanged in after-hours trading. The article highlights key factors influencing GS stock, including a broad "Santa rally," thin holiday trading liquidity, a data breach warning related to an outside law firm, and plans for significant expansion in Japan's mid-cap deals market. Investors are advised to watch for follow-up news on the data incident, deal-flow temperatures, rate expectations, and upcoming Q4 2025 earnings in mid-January.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Stock Touched a New 52-Week High: Is More Upside Left?
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company's shares recently hit a new 52-week high, driven by optimism about its entry into the options clearing business and the removal of a regulatory asset cap. The company's strategic moves, including business simplification, cost efficiency initiatives, and AI adoption, are expected to support its growth and profitability. Analysts anticipate further upside for the stock, especially with projected earnings rises and an "inexpensive" valuation relative to its industry.

feed.title: Pacasmayo (CPAC) Is Up 52.07% in One Week: What You Should Know
feed.summary: Pacasmayo (CPAC) has seen a significant price increase of 52.07% in one week, driven by strong momentum and positive earnings estimate revisions. The stock, currently holding a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and a Momentum Style Score of B, has outperformed its industry and the S&P 500 over various timeframes, indicating a promising outlook for investors.

feed.title: Tesla faces NHTSA probe over Model 3 emergency door handles
feed.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has launched a probe into the emergency door releases in certain Tesla Model 3 vehicles following a petition claiming they are "hidden, unlabeled, and not intuitive." This investigation covers approximately 179,071 Model 3 sedans from the 2022 model year and expands on previous scrutiny of Tesla's door systems, which has included reports of occupants being trapped after crashes. The move comes after a Tesla owner stated he had to kick his way out of his burning Model 3 due to difficulty locating the emergency release.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Just Opened Its New Bahamas Club on Paradise Island, With Three Pools and the World’s Largest Swim-Up Bar
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean has opened its new Royal Beach Club Paradise Island, an exclusive all-inclusive beach destination blending Bahamian culture with the cruise line's signature service. The club features three distinct zones including Party Cove with the world's largest swim-up bar, Chill Beach, and Family Beach, offering varied experiences for guests. This opening marks the first of Royal Caribbean's Royal Beach Club Collection, with another planned for Cozumel in 2026.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoran Inc. (FCX) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz are inviting shareholders of Freeport-McMoran Inc. (FCX) who suffered losses to join a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Freeport failed to disclose inadequate safety measures at its Grasberg Block Cave mine, leading to heightened risks for workers and misrepresenting the company's business prospects. The lead plaintiff deadline for the lawsuit is January 12, 2026.

feed.title: Better Home & Finance (NASDAQ:BETR) Stock Price Up 5.1% - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Better Home & Finance Holding Company (NASDAQ:BETR) saw its share price increase by 5.1% during Wednesday's trading, closing at $37.7510. The article details analyst rating changes, insider stock sales, and institutional ownership movements for the company.

feed.title: Mainland investors pour money into Hawaii self-storage market with at least six new projects
feed.summary: Mainland investors are heavily investing in Hawaii's self-storage market, with at least six new projects initiated this year. Developers are swiftly addressing a decades-long accumulated demand for self-storage facilities across the islands. The market leader, U-Haul and Extra Space Storage, are spearheading this expansion.

feed.title: 4 Retail Apparel Stocks Poised to Lead Consumer Rally in 2026
feed.summary: The retail apparel and footwear industry is expected to experience a major upcycle in 2026, driven by stabilizing interest rates, improving wage growth, and healthier inventory levels. Companies like American Eagle Outfitters, Urban Outfitters, Boot Barn, and The Gap are well-positioned to benefit from this consumer rally due to their strong brands, differentiated products, and agile omnichannel strategies. These firms are focusing on operational discipline, merchandising, and customer engagement to drive growth and profitability.

feed.title: Is Advance Auto Parts Recovering with New Leadership?
feed.summary: Advance Auto Parts Inc. (AAP) stocks have seen a 4.15% increase, driven by the appointment of Ronald Gilbert as senior VP of supply chain and insights from a recent DA Davidson conference call. While the company has shown mixed financial results including negative net income and ROE, Gilbert's leadership is expected to streamline operations and improve profitability. Investors are keenly watching for how these strategic changes will impact future performance and aid in the company's financial recovery.

feed.title: Deere & Company Snaps Six-Day Slide as Analysts Signal a Cyclical Floor Amid Industrial Sector Resurgence
feed.summary: Deere & Company (NYSE: DE) ended a six-day losing streak on December 24, 2025, with a modest rebound supported by bullish analyst ratings suggesting a cyclical bottom amidst a challenging fiscal year. This rebound, fueled by analysts affirming "Buy" and "Outperform" ratings and the strategic acquisition of Tenna, signals a potential shift in market sentiment towards a 2026 recovery in the industrial sector. The company is transitioning from a traditional manufacturer to a technology-first enterprise, focusing on precision agriculture and recurring software revenue to drive future growth.

feed.title: Citi’s Strategic Gambit: Inside the Aggressive Investment Banking Expansion Targeting a 2026 M&A Supercycle
feed.summary: Citigroup is aggressively expanding its investment banking division, pursuing a high-stakes talent acquisition strategy to capitalize on an anticipated M&A supercycle in 2026. This strategic pivot, marked by significant hires and a leaner management structure, aims to achieve an 11%-12% Return on Tangible Common Equity for 2026. The move is also positioning Citi to benefit from a broader industry shift towards growth and a focus on AI integration and energy transition deals.

feed.title: 3 Bank Stocks to Keep on Your Radar as They Reach New 52-Week Highs
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. (C), U.S. Bancorp (USB), and Bank of America Corporation (BAC) have recently reached new 52-week highs, driven by strong market performance, robust Q3 U.S. GDP growth, and anticipated interest rate cuts in 2026. These banks are also strengthening their long-term growth prospects through AI investments, digital capabilities, strategic acquisitions, and expanding their financial center networks. The article highlights reasons for continued upside potential for each of these three major bank stocks.

feed.title: Profund Medical Upsizes Private Placement to Raise US$6.45 Million
feed.summary: Profound Medical (TSE:PRN) has upsized its private placement to secure US$6.45 million by issuing 921,428 common shares at US$7.00 each, driven by demand from an existing investor. The capital will fuel expansion in sales, marketing, working capital, R&D, potential strategic transactions, and general corporate purposes, reinforcing the company's financial standing as it commercializes its therapeutic platforms. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates TSE:PRN as Neutral, highlighting strong revenue growth and strategic potential against operational inefficiencies and valuation concerns.

feed.title: TransDigm: The Moat Is Massive, The Margin Of Safety Is Not (NYSE:TDG)
feed.summary: TransDigm Group Incorporated (TDG) is recognized as a formidable aerospace compounder due to strong regulatory barriers, proprietary products, and effective decentralized management. While its high leverage amplifies returns, it also tightens the margin of safety, especially after recent debt-funded special dividends and a CEO transition. Despite these concerns and a valuation that doesn't offer a compelling entry for new investors, TDG remains a high-quality hold, supported by secular tailwinds and a fragmented aftermarket.

feed.title: Richmond firefighters battle large fire at Public Storage facility
feed.summary: Richmond firefighters are battling a large structure fire at a Public Storage facility located at 398 Carlson Blvd. The fire, which was reported Wednesday morning, is producing large plumes of smoke visible in the area. Authorities have closed Carlson Boulevard in the vicinity, but residents are not advised to shelter in place.

feed.title: Pentagon expands Lockheed Martin contract to $25 billion
feed.summary: The Pentagon has expanded its contract with Lockheed Martin, increasing the total value to $25 billion. This significant increase suggests a growing commitment to services provided by the defense contractor.

feed.title: Braskem’s Strategic Moves: Unlocking Market Potential?
feed.summary: Braskem SA ADR stocks saw an 8.96% increase, driven by strategic agreements with IG4 Capital, Novonor, and Petrobras. These moves aim to boost liquidity, secure investor trust, and enhance demand for Braskem's products, suggesting significant market potential and improved future prospects for the petrochemical titan. The company's financial metrics, including an enterprise value of $11.47B and revenue of $77.41B, underpin its strong market position despite substantial long-term debt.

feed.title: Sanofi’s Vaccine Lineup Gets a Shot in the Arm With $2.2B Dynavax Acquisition
feed.summary: Sanofi is acquiring Dynavax Technologies for $2.2 billion to bolster its vaccine portfolio, gaining a commercialized hepatitis B vaccine and a clinical-stage shingles vaccine. The acquisition aims to provide Sanofi with competitive advantages over rival GSK, particularly with Dynavax's two-injection hepatitis B vaccine compared to GSK's three-shot regimen and Dynavax's shingles vaccine candidate that showed fewer side effects than Shingrix. This marks Sanofi's second billion-dollar vaccine acquisition of 2025.

feed.title: Seagate Technology Hldgs Unusual Options Activity For December 24
feed.summary: Deep-pocketed investors have shown unusual options activity for Seagate Technology Holdings (NASDAQ: STX), indicating potential significant moves in the stock. While 36% of the notable options activity leaned bullish, 40% were bearish, with 2 puts totaling $99,790 and 23 calls amounting to $1,093,871. Analysts currently have an average target price of $327.33 for STX, with ratings ranging from Overweight to Buy.

feed.title: PepsiCo vs. Coca-Cola: Which Stock Dominates Global Beverage Space?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the rivalry between PepsiCo (PEP) and Coca-Cola (KO) in the global beverage market, comparing their business models, market positions, and financial performance. Coca-Cola, a pure-play beverage company, boasts unmatched scale and brand leadership, whereas PepsiCo blends beverages with a substantial snacks empire, offering diversification and competitive advantages. While KO has outperformed PEP in the past year and commands a higher valuation, PEP's lower P/E multiple and diversified revenue stream make it an attractive long-term holding.

feed.title: GM Names Jorge Plata CEO of Africa & Middle East
feed.summary: General Motors has appointed Jorge Plata, a Mexican executive, as the new president and CEO of General Motors Africa and Middle East, effective March 1, 2026. Plata, who has over 25 years of experience with GM, will relocate to Dubai and report to Michael MacPhee. This appointment is part of a broader executive transition within GM's international operations, with the former CEO, Jack Uppal, moving to GM Canada.

feed.title: Alphabet (GOOG) Stock After the Bell Today (Dec. 24, 2025): Google Class C Shares Hold Near $316 — Key News, Forecasts, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) closed essentially flat near $316 on a holiday-shortened Christmas Eve, as the broader market saw record highs. Key news influencing sentiment included Waymo's software updates post-San Francisco outage, a blocked Texas app-store age law, and ongoing debates about AI infrastructure spending returns. Investors are advised to watch for potential headline sensitivity due to thin holiday trading and to differentiate between GOOG and GOOGL ahead of the next full trading session on Friday, December 26, 2025.

feed.title: Saudi Aramco signs long-term agreement with SLB focused on unconventional gas development and operational efficiency in Saudi Arabia.
feed.summary: Saudi Aramco has signed a multi-billion dollar, five-year contract with SLB to accelerate unconventional gas projects, improve operational efficiency, and strengthen energy security in Saudi Arabia. This agreement is part of a broader program to diversify Saudi Aramco's energy mix, reduce reliance on oil, and support the global energy transition by leveraging SLB's advanced stimulation, automation, and digital solutions. The partnership aims to unlock the productive potential of unconventional gas safely and efficiently, aligning with Saudi Vision 2030.

feed.title: Top grocery chain 'cheaper than Dollar Tree' to open massive new store in DAYS
feed.summary: H-E-B, a popular grocery chain with over 400 locations, is set to open a new 127,000-square-foot store in northwest Bexar County, Texas, on January 7, 2026. A second location in the same county is planned for late 2026. The chain has been described by an influencer as "cheaper than Dollar Tree" due to its effective digital coupons, offering significant savings on groceries and household items.

feed.title: Intel Stock After Hours Today (Dec. 24, 2025): Nvidia’s 18A Test Report Drives Volatility — What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Intel's stock experienced volatility on December 24, 2025, primarily due to a Reuters report stating Nvidia tested Intel's 18A manufacturing process but "stopped moving forward." Despite a broader market rally and record highs for the Dow and S&P 500, the news created uncertainty around Intel's critical foundry strategy and its ability to attract top-tier customers. Investors are now focused on any forthcoming clarifications from Nvidia or Intel and how the stock will perform when markets reopen on Friday, December 26.

feed.title: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Faces Securities Class Action Amid Cybersecurity Incident, Questions About Disclosure Timing and Impact on Company's Business - Hagens Berman
feed.summary: F5, Inc. (FFIV) is facing a securities class action lawsuit filed by Hagens Berman on behalf of investors who purchased shares between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. The lawsuit stems from a cybersecurity incident involving a nation-state actor earlier in 2025 that gained unauthorized access to F5 systems, including its highest-revenue product, F5 BIG-IP. Investors suffered significant losses following disclosures about the breach and its material impact on the company's financial outlook, with Hagens Berman investigating whether F5 timely reported the breach to investors.

feed.title: Deere to Scale Customer-Focused Mixed-Fleet Business with Tenna Buyout
feed.summary: Deere & Company (DE) announced a deal to acquire Tenna, a construction technology company, to expand its customer-focused mixed-fleet business. Tenna provides a platform for equipment fleet operations that automates and optimizes construction workflows. The acquisition, expected to close in February 2026, will allow Tenna to continue operating independently while Deere scales its advanced technology offerings in the construction sector.

feed.title: How ExxonMobil Stays Resilient in a Soft Commodity Pricing Environment
feed.summary: ExxonMobil demonstrates resilience in a soft commodity pricing environment due to its low-breakeven Permian and Guyana assets and an integrated business model. The company also focuses on structural cost savings and maintains a strong balance sheet. Other energy companies like ConocoPhillips and EOG Resources also manage to thrive in challenging times with their low-cost, high-return assets.

feed.title: Citigroup (C) Stock After the Bell on Dec. 24, 2025: After-Hours Update, Today’s Key News, and What to Watch Before Markets Reopen
feed.summary: Citigroup (C) shares closed at $121.56 on a holiday-shortened Christmas Eve, up 1.8%, ending 2025 near its 52-week high. The stock's positive movement is attributed to easing regulatory burdens, with the OCC withdrawing an amendment to a 2020 consent order and the Fed terminating notices regarding trading risk management. Analysts are upgrading Citi, citing improved profitability and narrowing valuation discounts, as the bank prepares for its Q4 2025 earnings release on January 14, 2026.

feed.title: Banco De Chile (NYSE:BCH) Stock Price Down 6.7% - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Shares of Banco De Chile (NYSE:BCH) dropped 6.7% on Wednesday, with trading volume significantly below average. Analysts have a consensus "Hold" rating, though recent reports include target price increases from Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase & Co., and downgrades from Wall Street Zen and Zacks Research. The bank recently missed Q3 earnings and revenue estimates.

feed.title: PepsiCo vs. Coca-Cola: Which Stock Dominates Global Beverage Space?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the rivalry between PepsiCo (PEP) and Coca-Cola (KO) in the global beverage market, highlighting their distinct business models and market positions. While Coca-Cola focuses solely on beverages with a strong brand licensing model, PepsiCo combines beverages with a massive snack empire, offering a diversified approach. The piece compares their investment cases, price performance, valuation, and Zacks Consensus Estimates to determine which stock holds a near-term advantage and which offers a more compelling long-term holding.

feed.title: PG&E Co. CEO promises to compensate S.F. businesses for loss of revenue from blackout
feed.summary: Incoming PG&E Co. CEO Summeet Singh has announced that businesses affected by the recent blackout in San Francisco will be eligible for compensation. This compensation aims to cover some of the profits lost during the major holiday shopping period caused by the power outage. The blackout stemmed from a fire at PG&E's Mission substation, which impacted approximately 130,000 customers.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Cruises Sees Unusually High Options Volume (NYSE:RCL)
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Cruises (NYSE:RCL) experienced unusually high options trading volume on Wednesday, with traders acquiring 53,141 call options, a 721% increase from the typical daily volume. This surge in activity follows the company's announcement of a $2.00 billion stock buyback, a quarterly dividend of $1.00 per share, and strong Q3 earnings that beat analyst estimates. The cruise giant also provided upbeat FY2025 guidance, reinforcing a "Moderate Buy" analyst consensus with an average price target of $324.82.

feed.title: Altria Group Sees Unusually Large Options Volume (NYSE:MO)
feed.summary: Altria Group (NYSE:MO) recently experienced an unusually large options volume with traders acquiring 129,220 call options, a 278% increase from the daily average, indicating significant bullish activity. The company also announced a $2.0 billion share buyback program and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.06 per share, representing a 7.2% yield. Altria slightly surpassed Q3 EPS estimates and provided FY2025 guidance, maintaining an average analyst rating of "Hold" with a target price of $62.33.

feed.title: Hack of Aflac potentially affected more than 22 million people, company says
feed.summary: A cybersecurity breach at Columbus-based insurance giant Aflac Inc. potentially exposed the personal information of about 22.65 million people, the company reported last week. The exposed data included names, contact details, claims, health information, and Social Security numbers. Aflac detected suspicious activity in June related to a sophisticated campaign by a cybercrime group affecting numerous insurance companies and is offering identity theft protection services to affected individuals.

feed.title: Dollar Tree comes to Lanett
feed.summary: Dollar Tree is set to open a new location in Lanett, Alabama, in February 2026, occupying the former Colorvision site. This move is expected to bring low-cost shopping options, create jobs, and increase city revenue, with Lanett Mayor Jamie Heard noting a mixed but largely positive public reception. The store's accessible location and the city's integrated utility billing system made Lanett an attractive choice for Dollar Tree.

feed.title: AG Yost Joins Multistate Lawsuit Against Uber for Deceptive Subscription Practices
feed.summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has joined a multistate lawsuit against Uber USA, LLC and Uber Technologies, Inc. for deceptive practices related to its Uber One subscription service. The lawsuit alleges Uber misled customers with automatic enrollment into paid subscriptions after "free trials," exaggerated savings, made cancellations difficult, and charged some customers prematurely. The coalition seeks refunds for affected consumers, civil penalties, and an injunction against Uber's alleged deceptive tactics.

feed.title: Advance Auto Parts Stock (NYSE: AAP) on Dec. 24, 2025: Latest News, Turnaround Forecasts, and Analyst Outlook
feed.summary: Advance Auto Parts (NYSE: AAP) is navigating a volatile period, with its stock influenced by a recent supply chain leadership change, sector-wide pricing concerns, and its ongoing turnaround strategy. The company's updated FY2025 guidance reflects improving profitability but continued negative free cash flow, while analyst ratings show mixed sentiment with varying price targets. Investors are closely watching for concrete evidence of cash flow generation and the sustained success of its operational restructuring efforts.

feed.title: What's Driving the Market Sentiment Around Hyperscale Data Inc?
feed.summary: Hyperscale Data Inc.'s short interest has recently decreased by 19.06%, now standing at 17.30 million shares, or 5.35% of its tradable shares. This reduction suggests a potentially more bullish sentiment among investors. Despite the drop, Hyperscale Data Inc. still has a higher short interest percentage compared to its peer group average of 4.76%.

feed.title: Plug Power vs. Ballard Power: Which Fuel Cell Stock has Greater Upside?
feed.summary: This article compares Plug Power (PLUG) and Ballard Power Systems (BLDP), two prominent fuel cell companies, to determine which has greater upside potential. While Plug Power shows growth in green hydrogen but struggles with negative gross margins and cash flow, Ballard Power benefits from strong order intakes, product innovation, and a healthy liquidity position. Based on current fundamentals and growth prospects, the analysis concludes that Ballard Power appears to be a better investment choice than Plug Power.

feed.title: Profound Medical Corp. Announces Upsize of Private Placement
feed.summary: Profound Medical Corp. announced an upsizing of its previously announced private placement to accommodate an existing long-term investor. The company now expects to raise up to US$6.45 million through the issuance of 921,428 common shares at US$7.00 per share, with the closing anticipated on or before December 30, 2025. The net proceeds will be used for sales and marketing expansion, working capital, research and development, strategic transactions, and general corporate purposes.

feed.title: AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) CFO Dennis Mulroy Sells 10,000 Shares
feed.summary: AnaptysBio CFO Dennis Mulroy sold 10,000 shares of ANAB stock for $500,000 on December 22, and an additional 15,725 shares on December 16, significantly reducing his stake. Despite Mulroy's sales, AnaptysBio beat earnings expectations, authorized a $100 million share buyback, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $55.40. The company's stock shows strong year-to-date performance but remains unprofitable.

feed.title: Honeywell flags $470mn potential liability over Flexjet suit
feed.summary: Honeywell International Inc. has disclosed a potential liability of $470 million stemming from a 2019 lawsuit filed by Flexjet. This amount represents an accounting recognition, and Flexjet states the final settlement could exceed $1 billion, arising from an agreement where Honeywell was to service and maintain engines across Flexjet's fleet. Flexjet is encouraged by Honeywell's public acknowledgment and is seeking a resolution aligned with their Master Services Agreement.

feed.title: SLB Secures Multi-Year Deal to Boost Unconventional Gas Output
feed.summary: SLB N.V. secured a multi-year contract from Aramco to enhance Saudi Arabia’s unconventional gas production, aligning with Saudi Vision 2030 and SLB's energy transition strategy. The agreement will see SLB provide advanced services and technologies to improve extraction efficiency from difficult-to-access reserves. This deal is expected to strengthen SLB's order backlog and cash flow, despite the company's current vulnerability to crude oil price volatility.

feed.title: DEFT Shareholders - If you lost money in your DeFi Technologies Inc. investment contact Robbins LLP about leading the class DEFT class action
feed.summary: Robbins LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against DeFi Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:DEFT) on behalf of investors who purchased shares between May 12 and November 14, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that the company misled investors by failing to disclose delays in its DeFi arbitrage strategy, understating competition, and overstating revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025. Following a significant revenue decline and reduced forecast in Q3 2025, and the CEO's departure, the stock price dropped by over 27%.

feed.title: AU vs. NEM: Which Gold Mining Stock Is the Better Buy Right Now?
feed.summary: AngloGold Ashanti (AU) and Newmont Corporation (NEM) are prominent gold producers benefiting from rising gold prices due to safe-haven demand and geopolitical risks. While both companies show strong potential, AngloGold Ashanti is highlighted as the more compelling investment choice due to its stronger one-year price performance and more attractive valuation. The article examines their fundamentals, growth prospects, and challenges to justify this conclusion, noting that both hold a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).
